verses.text: A wise son hears his father's instruction, but a scorner heeds no rebuke; a man eats good by his words' fruit, and 'he that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life.' The tongue holds life and death. The wise guard their words and welcome correction.

A wise son hears his father's instruction, but a scorner heeds no rebuke; a man eats good by his words' fruit, and 'he that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life.' The tongue holds life and death. The wise guard their words and welcome correction.

A wise son hears his father's instruction, but a scorner heeds no rebuke; a man eats good by his words' fruit, and 'he that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life.' The tongue holds life and death. The wise guard their words and welcome correction.

The sluggard desires and has nothing, but the diligent are made fat; a righteous man hates lying, and righteousness keeps the upright, while wickedness overthrows the sinner. Diligence and integrity prosper. God's righteousness is a guard, but sin trips its own.

The sluggard desires and has nothing, but the diligent are made fat; a righteous man hates lying, and righteousness keeps the upright, while wickedness overthrows the sinner. Diligence and integrity prosper. God's righteousness is a guard, but sin trips its own.

The sluggard desires and has nothing, but the diligent are made fat; a righteous man hates lying, and righteousness keeps the upright, while wickedness overthrows the sinner. Diligence and integrity prosper. God's righteousness is a guard, but sin trips its own.

Some pretend rich yet have nothing, others poor yet have much; 'the light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.' Appearances deceive. The joy of the godly shines on, while the wicked's brightness is snuffed out.

Some pretend rich yet have nothing, others poor yet have much; 'the light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.' Appearances deceive. The joy of the godly shines on, while the wicked's brightness is snuffed out.

Only by pride comes contention, but with the well-advised is wisdom; wealth gotten by vanity dwindles, but gathered by labour increases; 'hope deferred maketh the heart sick.' Patience and humility profit. Honest gain endures, and longing fulfilled is a tree of life.

He that despises the word is destroyed, but he that fears the commandment is rewarded; the law of the wise is a fountain of life; 'good understanding giveth favour, but the way of transgressors is hard.' God's word is life. To honour it is blessing; to despise it, ruin.

Every prudent man acts with knowledge, but a fool lays open his folly; a wicked messenger falls into mischief, while a faithful one is health; 'poverty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction.' Wisdom acts carefully. Those who heed correction find honour.

Desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools hate to depart from evil; 'he that walketh with wise men shall be wise,' but a companion of fools is destroyed, and evil pursues sinners. Company shapes character. Walk with the wise, for sin hunts down the foolish.

A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, and the sinner's wealth is laid up for the just; he that spares the rod hates his son, but the loving father chastens; 'the righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul.' Diligence and discipline bless a household. God gives the righteous enough.
